title: Engine.Launch mutates shared e.deps.OnBoot with only a comment enforcing the fresh-engine-per-Launch contract — make Deps call-local so concurrent Launch on one Engine is structurally safe
summary: bridge/engine.go:429-436: Launch saves prevOnBoot, writes a call-local closure into e.deps.OnBoot, and defer-restores it. The safety contract ('NOT safe for concurrent calls on the same Engine', engine.go:333-334) is enforced only by adapters/bridge building a fresh Engine per call — a comment in a different file. Any future engine-caching refactor (natural on THE hot path every LLM call funnels through) silently introduces a data race; no concurrent-Launch test exists to catch it.
root_cause: Call-scoped state stored on the shared receiver; invariant lives in prose instead of structure.
fix: Copy deps at Launch entry (deps := e.deps), install the OnBoot wrapper on the copy, and thread the copy through LaunchArgs (accept Deps as a parameter instead of reading e.deps). Delete the defer-restore. e.deps becomes immutable after construction; the comment contract becomes unnecessary. CARE: engine.go was instrumented by token-telemetry S3 (llm-calls.ndjson appender) — rebase around that seam, do not disturb the collector call.
acceptance: RED: TestEngine_ConcurrentLaunchOnSameInstance — two Launches on ONE Engine under -race with distinct OnBoot observations; no race, each Launch sees its own bootMS (fails/races today)
Existing Launch behavior byte-identical (bridge suite green, llm-calls.ndjson record still emitted per attempt)
operator_note: LANDED 94c01841 (2026-07-14, operator TDD): Launch installs the OnBoot hook on a per-call Deps copy and runs the pipeline against a scoped Engine{deps:copy}; RED test TestEngine_ConcurrentLaunch_OnBootRaceFree (data race on old code) now GREEN under -race. Closing.
